rulings.Case Title: SCOTT FLETCHER VS. BRUSCO TUG & BARGE, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 15, 2009, line 3, PLAINTIFF SCOTT FLETCHER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion must be, and it is, denied. The motion violates CRC Rule 3.1350(b) & (d). Those are not minor procedural quibles. CCP section 437c(f)(1) allows summary adjudication of a cause of action, only if it completely disposes of a cause of action. Plaintiff furnishes no proof as to his damages. Neither does plaintiff's showing dispose of the contributory negligence and comparative fault affirmative defenses. He fails to show violation of 46 USC 8104(e). Defendant's tugboat is not "a merchant vessel," it's a "towing vessel" per 46 USC 8104(c) and (g) and subsection (g) is permissive. Under subsection (h), which does control tugboats the licensed captain could serve as the watchman for up to twelve hours. Finally, Mathes vs. The Clipper Fleet (9 Cir. 1985) 774 F.2nd 980, 982-983 explains there must be nexus between statutory breach and inquiry; Defendant's evidentiary objection to the Sharpe Declaration is sustained. This Court, not Mr. Sharpe, will continue to decide legal issues. =(302KOPP/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH LINK VS. PHD HOLDINGS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 15, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT PHD HOLDINGS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, and, JAMES ROTH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is overruled. Its attempted demurrer to the 12th Cause of Action violates CRC Rule 3.1320(a). Its notice relative to the 11th cause of action is deflective. The 12th cause of action alleges constructive eviction; the 11th (mentioned in its notice) does not. Moreover, Plaintiff does state a cause of action for constructive eviction and is not barred by the limitation of actions. Defendant shall file and serve its answer by April 27, 2009. =(302KOPP/JW)

rulings.Case Title: PETER CHILDRESS VS. WILLIAM MURPHY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant William Murphy'S Motion To Strike Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 15, 2009, line 13, Defendant William Murphy's Motion to Strike Complaint. The motion is denied. Plaintiff sues for imposition of a trust regarding the subject property. The now deleted paragraph 29 does not alter the true character of the action. Moreover, paragraph 29 is moot as of April 1, 2009. =(302KOPP)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA NATIONAL BANK AS TRUSTEE FOR THE VS. MONTGOMERY REALTY GROUP, INC A NEVADA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Bank Of America National Bank'S Order To Show Cause Why Appointment Of Receiver Should Not Be Confirmed And Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Granted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 15, 2009, line 9, Plaintiff Bank of America National Bank's Order to Show Cause why Appointment of Receiver should not be confirmed and why preliminary injunction should not be granted (Per ex parte Order of 3/30/09). The subject deed of trust expressly confers on the Plaintiff the right to obtain a receiver "as a matter of strict right" and regardless of the adequacy of the security to pay the loan. [Clause 7.3(b), Deed of Trust]. Defendant's default is undisputed. A receiver shall be appointed. The Court recommends Mr. Randy Sugarman. Defendant's two evidentiary objections are sustained. =(302KOPP/VC)
